title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA Keylogger Component (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B)
description: Detects BLUELIGHT keylogger component strings. The malware logs keystrokes with modifier prefixes (SHIFT+, CTRL+, CAPSLOCK+, ALT+) and special key markers ([TAB], [RETURN], [ESC], [ARROW *], [DEL], [WIN]). The keylogger output is staged for exfiltration via OneDrive. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $keylogger_component strings (27 of 29 required).
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and ((('Target Filename' like '*.dat' or 'Target Filename' like '*.log' or 'Target Filename' like '*.tmp') and ('Target Filename' like '*cheV01*' or 'Target Filename' like '*INTEG.RAW*' or 'Target Filename' like '*keylog*')) or (('Target Filename' like '*edb.chk*' or 'Target Filename' like '*edb.log*' or 'Target Filename' like '*edbres00001.jrs*' or 'Target Filename' like '*edbres00002.jrs*' or 'Target Filename' like '*edbtmp.log*'))) | stats count by 'Host Name (Server)', Entity, 'Process Name'
falsepositives: Windows Exchange/ESE database operations create edb.* files legitimately
The cheV01.dat file is unique to BLUELIGHT
